query: I want to reduce long-context decoding memory without changing model weights. Retrieve papers with KV-cache compression mechanisms, retention-budget assumptions, implementation sanity checks, and evidence about memory, latency, sequence length, and quality tradeoffs.
expected_context_properties: Context should route to KV-cache compression rather than quantization or generic long-context summaries.
Context should expose implementation checks for retention ratio, tensor shapes, cache budget, sequence length, memory, latency, and quality.
Adjacent attention-kernel papers are acceptable only as systems/runtime context, not as the primary KV-cache method.
must_not_do: Do not retrieve weight/activation quantization as the top result.
Do not treat context extrapolation alone as sufficient evidence for decode-memory reduction.
judge_rubric: Top result should be PyramidKV or another KV-cache compression page.
Matched sections should include mechanism, implementation hooks, and evidence/scope signals.
Distractors should not outrank the required KV-cache page.

query: I want to compare preference optimization with test-time reinforcement learning for improving language-model behavior. Retrieve papers that separate preference-pair objectives, reference-policy or KL assumptions, reward/evaluation signals, rollout or test-time update evidence, and limitations of each training or inference regime.
expected_context_properties: Context should distinguish preference optimization from generic RL and from test-time RL.
Context should surface objective assumptions, evaluation evidence, and implementation hooks for both regimes.
Survey pages can appear only as adjacent taxonomy context.
must_not_do: Do not collapse DPO into generic benchmark work.
Do not retrieve quantization or attention-kernel papers as primary evidence.
judge_rubric: Both DPO and TTRL families should be present in top-k.
The packet should make the comparison explainable from frontmatter and matched sections.
If only one family is retrieved, mark recall failure even if the top result is relevant.

query: I want to connect a clustering objective to representation learning. Retrieve papers that help reason about centroid assignments, PCA or objective assumptions, representation probes, downstream transfer, ablations, and sanity checks for whether clustering structure is actually helping learned representations.
expected_context_properties: Context should retrieve clustering theory without being polluted by non-uniform quantization or KV-cache position selection.
Representation-learning pages are acceptable adjacent context if they expose probes or downstream transfer assumptions.
The packet should help design sanity checks around centroid updates, objective assumptions, and representation usefulness.
must_not_do: Do not treat quantization centroid/codebook papers as the primary clustering objective evidence.
Do not route KV-cache selecting/clustering of token positions as clustering theory.
judge_rubric: Required clustering page must appear and should not be outranked by quantization centroid pages.
Matched sections should include mechanism and implementation hooks.
Adjacent representation learning pages should be clearly adjacent, not substitutes for clustering theory.

query: I want to build speculative execution for agent workflows. Retrieve papers about action-level speculation, verifier or ground-truth executor behavior, rollback/commit semantics, tool or environment state, latency, and task-success preservation; also retrieve any survey page that helps place this mechanism in the broader LLM-agent workflow taxonomy.
expected_context_properties: Context should treat this as action-level agent speculation, not token-level speculative decoding.
Context should retrieve rollback, verifier, environment/tool state, latency, and task-success checks.
Survey page is useful if it gives taxonomy context for planning/memory/action/evaluation modules.
must_not_do: Do not retrieve speculative decoding or attention-kernel papers as the main answer.
Do not collapse agent workflow execution into generic benchmark acceleration.
judge_rubric: Speculative Actions must be top-ranked or near top-ranked.
Matched sections should support implementation planning and failure-boundary review.
The packet should explain why survey context is adjacent rather than primary.

query: I want to design an audio-language model experiment that separates audio preprocessing, encoder-language alignment, decoder prompting, task tags, and evaluation across speech, music, sound events, and audio question answering. Retrieve papers and sections that expose multimodal mechanism contracts and implementation probes.
expected_context_properties: Context should route to audio-language modeling rather than generic transformer architecture.
Evidence should be task-family aware and should not mix speech, music, sound, and audio-QA into one uninspected score.
Adjacent representation-learning pages are acceptable only for multimodal representation design, not as audio evidence.
must_not_do: Do not retrieve preference optimization as the primary route just because evaluation or instruction tuning appears.
Do not treat audio-language alignment as generic sequence modeling.
judge_rubric: Qwen-Audio must be retrieved with mechanism and implementation hook sections.
Routing should expose audio encoder alignment, multimodal instruction setting, or audio-language task families.
Distractors should not be top result.

query: I want to use neural networks for PDE-constrained scientific modeling. Retrieve papers that explain PDE residual losses, boundary or initial conditions, collocation sampling, forward versus inverse problem evidence, implementation tests for autodiff shapes, and limitations around residual weighting or physical-system mismatch.
expected_context_properties: Context should retrieve scientific ML/PINN pages without RL, quantization, or agent contamination.
Implementation hooks should mention PDE residual computation, collocation sampling, boundary/initial-condition losses, and data/physics loss separation.
Evidence should distinguish forward solution accuracy from inverse parameter identification.
must_not_do: Do not route the query to RL only because the word policy or optimization appears in the corpus.
Do not use generic neural-network architecture pages as a substitute for PDE constraints.
judge_rubric: PINN page must be top-ranked or clearly present.
Matched sections should include mechanism, implementation hooks, and limitations.
No declared distractor should outrank the required scientific ML page.
